title: Mental Math Training Game - Frontend Implementation

requirements.summary: Build game screen with 2-minute countdown timer, problem display, answer input, and scoring logic
acceptanceCriteria: Countdown timer starts at 2:00 and counts down in real-time seconds
One problem is shown at a time
Correct answer increments score and loads next problem instantly
Incorrect answer loads next problem without incrementing score
Game ends automatically when timer hits 0:00
Final score screen shows total correct answers

file_operations.description: Create the main game screen component that manages the 2-minute countdown timer (starting at 120 seconds), fetches problems from backend via generateProblem(), displays the current problem question, accepts numeric answer input, validates answers against correctAnswer, increments score on correct answers, loads next problem immediately after submission, and transitions to results screen when timer reaches zero. Use the backend interface types (Problem, Operation) for type safety.

Create a custom hook that manages the 2-minute countdown timer logic, starting at 120 seconds, decrementing every second using setInterval, providing current time remaining, and triggering a callback when time reaches zero to end the game session.

Create a custom hook that manages game state including current score (number of correct answers), current problem (fetched via generateProblem backend function), answer input value, submission logic that compares input to correctAnswer, score increment on correct answer, and loading the next problem. Return state and handlers for use in GameScreen component.
Add route for GameScreen and wire navigation from StartScreen to GameScreen and from GameScreen to ResultsScreen based on game state transitions.

requirements.summary: Build start screen with game rules explanation and Start Game button
acceptanceCriteria: Start screen displays game name, brief instructions (2 minutes, answer as many as you can, all four operations)
A prominent 'Start Game' button transitions to the active game screen

file_operations.description: Create the start screen component that displays the game name 'MathSprint', brief instructions explaining the 2-minute time limit and all four operations (+, -, ×, ÷), and includes the generated logo image from frontend/public/assets/generated/mathsprint-logo.dim_256x256.png. Provide a prominent 'Start Game' button that triggers navigation to the game screen.
Add route for StartScreen as the default/home route and wire the Start Game button navigation to GameScreen.

requirements.summary: Build results screen showing final score, leaderboard submission, top-10 display, and Play Again button
acceptanceCriteria: Results screen shows total correct answers prominently
Player can enter their name and submit score
Top-10 leaderboard is displayed with rank, name, and score
Play Again button resets the game to start screen

file_operations.description: Create the results screen component that displays the final score (number of correct answers) prominently, provides a name input field and submit button to call submitScore backend function with the player name and score, fetches and displays the top-10 leaderboard via getLeaderboard backend function showing rank, name, and numCorrect for each entry, and includes a 'Play Again' button that navigates back to the start screen. Use backend interface types (Score) for type safety.
Add route for ResultsScreen and wire the Play Again button navigation back to StartScreen.

requirements.summary: Apply bold, energetic visual theme with dark background, bright accents, large typography, and smooth transitions
acceptanceCriteria: Dark background with high-contrast accent colors applied consistently across all screens
Problem text is large and instantly readable
Score and timer are always prominently visible during gameplay

file_operations.description: Update global styles to apply a dark background (e.g., near-black #0a0a0a or similar) with neon green (#00ff88 or similar) and vivid orange (#ff6b35 or similar) accent colors. Ensure high contrast for readability and apply consistent theming across all screens.

Extend Tailwind theme configuration to include custom colors for the energetic theme (dark background, neon green accent, vivid orange accent), configure large font sizes for problem text (e.g., text-5xl or text-6xl), and add transition utilities with duration under 150ms for snappy problem transitions.
Create a layout component that wraps all screens with the dark background theme and ensures consistent styling. This component provides the visual frame for start, game, and results screens.
Wrap all routes with the Layout component to ensure consistent theming across StartScreen, GameScreen, and ResultsScreen.
